name: CadDataFileReader
summary: Utility for reading data from common CAD files, like IFC or DWG, and converting them into Python objects (like ModelElement3D), but without creating these objects in the drawing file. In this way, the objects can be processed further inside the script e.g., become a part of the created PythonPart.

methods.name: ReadIFC
methods.signature: ReadIFC( doc: DocumentAdapter, fileName: str ) -> list[tuple[str, list[ModelElement3D]]]
methods.summary: Import the data from an IFC file and return it as Python objects, without creating them in the drawing file. The method groups objects, that are sub objects of an IfcElementAssembly, into tuples. If there is no IfcElementAssembly entity in the IFC file, one tuple with all objects is returned. IMPORTANT: Only objects with the IfcType of IfcBuildingElementProxy are read!
methods.remarks: Import the data from an IFC file and return it as Python objects, without creating them in the drawing file. The method groups objects, that are sub objects of an IfcElementAssembly, into tuples. If there is no IfcElementAssembly entity in the IFC file, one tuple with all objects is returned. IMPORTANT: Only objects with the IfcType of IfcBuildingElementProxy are read!

params.name: fileName
params.doc: Full path to the IFC file

methods.name: ReadOBJ
methods.signature: ReadOBJ( fileName: str, designPathLocation: eDesignPathLocation ) -> list[ModelElement3D]

params.name: fileName
params.doc: Full file name of the OBJ file
